channel.title: Lab projects feed
channel.description: Riley J. Shaw is a programmer and designer. Rooted in repair and craft, he creates transparent, remixable tools for a sustainable and accessible future.

item.title: bowen.mov
item.description: waves will wash it away

item.title: Gridstrument tutorial
item.description: A quick walkthrough of my Gridstrument config for the Intech Grid BU16 and VSN1 modules.

item.title: Gridstrument preview
item.description: I wrote some firmware for the Intech BU16. You can find it under the public "Gridstrument" folder, or by using this link: grid-editor://?config-link=R8adbrWIyNGzXtcQM8s4

item.title: Rephase
item.description: Reframe a tiling image by picking a new origin.

item.title: A few interesting rules
item.description: I found these patterns using https://rileyjshaw.com/ca-finder. No AI, just simple cellular automata.

item.title: Big Dipper
item.description: I built a hardware sequencer out of DIP switches in 2018. Now it’s playable on the web.

item.title: Terminal 1
item.description: A playful contribution to 10k Drum Machines based on the Collatz conjecture.

item.title: sick day
item.description: A little sketch using two new instruments I made.

item.title: Channel delay
item.description: RGB channel delay video shader.

item.title: Cutup
item.description: A grid symmetry shader and camera.

item.title: spruce
item.description: more tenori-on practice

item.title: cassiopeia
item.description: tenori-on practice

item.title: acid rain
item.description: tenori-on practice. acid sounds are played in drambo, sequenced by pika blue’s rain mode.

item.title: lunar
item.description: linnstrument 128 + tenori-on

item.title: Shifter
item.description: Move yr head.

item.title: Sway
item.description: Variations on an old CSS animation I made, rewritten as a shader. Use arrow keys or swipe to change the scene.

item.title: One-shot sample generator
item.description: Render per-note audio samples from a local VST or AU instrument plugin.

item.title: Mandy’s
item.description: A salad dressing index for the book “Mandy’s Gourmet Salads”

item.title: Egg calendar
item.description: A calendar that looks like eggs

item.title: Choreography
item.description: Some fun demos of emoji choreography using spatial indexing.

item.title: Moiré shader
item.description: An interesting illusion occurs when moving two overlapping instances of white noise. Move your cursor to see the effect.

item.title: Harmonics
item.description: Caution: sometimes flashes! Generates a new randomized shader composed of stacked trigonometric functions on each page refresh.

item.title: ShaderPad
item.description: A lightweight, dependency-free library to reduce boilerplate when writing fragment shaders.

item.title: Tempo tracker
item.description: A simple tap tempo app with drift tracking over time.

item.title: CA Finder: GPU Edition
item.description: Caution: it flashes! A generalized cellular automaton simulator that runs on your GPU.

item.title: Cell spread
item.description: A simple ShaderToy cellular automaton.

item.title: Pulley
item.description: A fun way to display an ordered list. CSS only.

item.title: Rippling scroll delay
item.description: Scrolling any column will update the rest, but with a delay.

item.title: FFmpeg slit-scan
item.description: Create an ultra-wide photograph out of a slow dolly shot video using the command line.

item.title: Split scroll columns
item.description: When one goes up, the other comes down.

item.title: Spiral out
item.description: A simple text spiral.

item.title: 10 PRINT variations
item.description: Remixes of 10 PRINT using messy grids.

item.title: 10 PRXXT
item.description: A remix of 10 PRINT using an even messier grid.

item.title: Stargazer touch
item.description: This is a free, touchscreen version of my Stargazer MIDI controller. The default layout for...

item.title: Dim
item.description: A simple dark theme for Firefox.

item.title: Roll
item.description: Roll a D6.

item.title: Compare images
item.description: Compare two images in a variety of ways.

item.title: cnz
item.description: A really tiny library that generates classname strings.

item.title: John Cage - As Slow as Possible (St. Burchardi Church, Halberstadt)
item.description: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/As_Slow_as_Possible#Halberstadt_performance

item.title: Ribbon creator
item.description: An interactive version of my Ribbon doodle.

item.title: Ribbon
item.description: A doodle I made while eating Fruit by the Foot.

item.title: Glass rings
item.description:
More explorations with SVG.

In these demos, SVG noise is used as a filter to distort geometric canvas animations.

item.title: SVG filters
item.description: The SVG specification has built-in noise and lighting, so I tried them out. Infinitely scalable, non-repeating patterns in much less than a kilobyte.

item.title: Great Fairy’s Fountain
item.description: “Hey, listen!” 🧚‍♀️

item.title: G Minor 9
item.description: A quick doodle after work today.

item.title: MIDI grid
item.description: I made a new instrument!

item.title: My keyboard layout
item.description: The layout I use for my Moonlander keyboard.

item.title: Marqueee (rotated)
item.description: A remix of Marqueee, with a rotate parameter added.

item.title: CSV to GPX
item.description: Converts a CSV file to a Garmin-flavored .gpx file.

item.title: Variable font: headings grow on hover
item.description: Another quick experiment with variable fonts following LOUDER. The heading text grows in response to hover or touch proximity.

item.title: LFOs
item.description: Set and toggle 16 independent LFOs. You can cross-modulate them if you want, but by default it’s just a bunch of LFOs.

item.title: LOUDER
item.description: A quick experiment with variable fonts. Responds to mouse or touch.

item.title: Power Song Finder
item.description: Searches your favorite Spotify tracks for a running “Power Song”. A Power Song matches your target cadence, and pumps you up during a tough point in the run.

item.title: Pocket calculator
item.description: Scrapes your unread and archived Pocket content and provides basic stats.

item.title: Scribbler
item.description: Combine many types of lines into a single line.

item.title: Gravity delay
item.description: This is a creative MIDI delay that physically models incoming notes based on their velocity...

item.title: Panning gravity delay
item.description: This is a creative MIDI delay that physically models incoming notes based on their velocity...

item.title: Mozaic demos: Gravity delay, panning gravity delay, dedupe
item.description: A quick demo of three Mozaic plugins I wrote in December 2020: “Gravity delay”, “Panning gravity delay”, and “Dedupe”. Download them for free on Patchstorage:

item.title: SVG <animate> tests
item.description: Even more tiny tests while learning SVG’s SMIL specification.

item.title: breathing timer
item.description:
A quick experiment to see if SVG <animateTransform> declarations can be copied and reused (they can).

Inhale for 4 seconds, hold for 7 seconds, exhale for 9 seconds.

item.title: Minimal SVG dark mode icon
item.description:
An attempt to fit a few difficult SVG requirements (SMIL animation, resizable with fixed stroke width, fits within container bounds) into the same icon.

It’s a different icon than the one I created for this site.

item.title: Winking SVG
item.description:
A raw SVG animation using no external libraries. It’s also cute. Click to animate.

Used as an initial test for this site’s theme picker icon.

item.title: Palette test tool
item.description:
Bikeshedding (noun): Futile investment of time and energy in discussion of marginal technical issues. Procrastination.

A bespoke developer tool that I created to choose the additive (dark theme) and subtractive (light theme) color palettes for this website.

item.title: Font scout
item.description:
A tool for comparing local / web fonts side-by-side.

This wasn’t designed for public consumption, but the code is open-source if you want to remix it with your own fonts. I recorded a quick session of myself using it if you’re curious.

item.title: Canvas smudge effect demos
item.description: Reusable functions that accept an HTML canvas and re-draw it slightly smudged. The CMY version separates cyan/magenta/yellow color channels and adds an offset.

item.title: MIDI Channel Shifter
item.description: Shifts the MIDI Channel of incoming MIDI messages. Knob 1: Channel shift amount, from -15...

item.title: Dedupe
item.description: Dedupe prevents consecutive MIDI Note On messages from occurring on the same note & channel...

item.title: Jambo brightness control demo
item.description: Bit of progress as I work on the settings menu :)

item.title: Panic! At The Drambo
item.description: This is a MIDI panic rack. I've been using Drambo more and more to sequence...

item.title: MIDI note # to pitch conversion
item.description: This demo rack uses a few Math modules to convert an integer MIDI note number...

item.title: Panic! At The Drambo (MIDI panic rack)
item.description: This is a MIDI panic rack.

item.title: Glitch More
item.description: A first pass at recreating my favourite parts of Alex Matheu's lovely GlitchCore app in...

item.title: Glitch More
item.description: A first pass at recreating my favourite parts of Alex Matheu's lovely GlitchCore app in Drambo. You can download it on PatchStorage here: https://patchstorage.com/glitch-more/

item.title: resurfacing
item.description: sfpc spring 2018 showcase project

item.title: Jambo MIDI map
item.description: A simple MIDI mapping for my Jambo hardware controller. The DIY guide is up at...

item.title: Scaling faders (audio demo)
item.description:
I dread mixing audio. I tend to make the drums a little louder, then the lead, then the bass, then the drums again, and on and on until I’m clipping everywhere.

I made this demo UI to show how a mixer might help keep your levels in check. Does this exist yet?

item.title: Seg16
item.description: A simple Arduino library for driving 16-segment displays with a HT16K33.

item.title: Chorder!
item.description: A parameter-lockable, MIDI-mappable converter from a single MIDI note to 8 configurable chords. For my...

item.title: Making a chord progression on the fly w/ Chorder! (v1.0.0)
item.description: This is a demo video for my Drambo “Chorder!” rack, a parameter-lockable, MIDI-mappable converter from a single MIDI note to 8 configurable chords.

item.title: canvas b/w image effects
item.description: A few quick doodles that I eventually ported to WebGL.

item.title: Jambo v1.0.0
item.description: UPDATE: I created an 18-page handwritten DIY guide. It’s for sale at https://gum.co/jambo.

item.title: Double double dots (CSS only)
item.description: Recursive illustration of the loci of points on a moving circle.

item.title: Deletion Day
item.description: Deletion Day challenges the prevailing notion that “more is more”. In a culture that strives for permanence, we celebrate ephemerality, growth, and change.

item.title: Full-width text experiments
item.description: Typesetting experiments in stretching, smooshing, and justification.

item.title: CSS vertical rhythm
item.description: An adaptive ruleset for vertical rhythm, responsive to the font-size of the parent container.

item.title: Neumorphic keyboard
item.description: Hopping on the bandwagon…

item.title: PO-33 loader
item.description: A drum sample loader for my Pocket Operator K.O.

item.title: Spiral explorer
item.description: A WIP / DOA sandbox for exploring single-line, tiling spirals.

item.title: Scroll-snap grid
item.description:
Prototype for a scrollable full-screen gallery using CSS scroll-snap- properties.

Works with touch, touchpad, mouse, and arrow keys.

item.title: timestamped
item.description: This video played behind me during my Winter 2018 School for Poetic Computation intro. The following is a transcript of my talk:

item.title: Plop
item.description: A scriptable text expansion microtool.

item.title: SVG text: subtractive color blend with mix-blend-mode: multiply
Marqueee
item.description:
A quick experiment to render an efficient marquee / ticker tape on a canvas.

Used as part of an online treasure hunt I made.

item.title: Servo
item.description: A fork of the official Arduino Servo library, improved to match standard R/C pulse widths.

item.title: ascii garden
item.description: An ascii rock garden. Rake with touch / mouse.

item.title: txt flowers
item.description: A garden of emoji that slowly climbs your screen.

item.title: typing lazy (desktop only)
item.description:
Try touch-typing an English word without moving your fingers from their resting position on the keyboard.

This was a proof-of-concept for a keyboard glove, which was implemented in hardware by mapping the trie to a directory tree.

item.title: CA finder
item.description: Caution: it flashes! Controls listed in the README.

item.title: 1.618 tests
item.description: After watching some Vi Hart videos, I started thinking a lot about Fibonacci Hashing and made some simple visualizations to test ideas.

item.title: b/w?
item.description: Testing the limits of the illusion that R+G+B = grey

item.title: Mystery house
item.description: A house built collaboratively in MagicaVoxel with my friend Hannah.

item.title: Stipphonic
item.description:
I wanted to see what stippling sounded like applied to musical chords.

Adapted from Virtuoso.

item.title: smudge
item.description: Created in 99B of Javascript.

item.title: Huertbeat
item.description: Pulse available Philips hue lights to whatever is playing on Spotify.

item.title: Huemidity
item.description: Control your Philips Hue lights with MIDI triggers.

item.title: Rainbobble
item.description: Rainbow remix of Bubble.

item.title: canvas
item.description:
THIRTY-EIGHT rough visual experiments in alphabetical order.

Each experiment was built using a web tool I created in the spirit of OpenFrameworks and TouchDesigner. I wanted libraries close at hand with a simple API for drawing to <canvas>, SVG, etc.

item.title: Photo experiment
item.description:
Combines two images by alternating them row-by-row.

Each row is placed to line up visually with the row above it, which causes a subtle shearing effect.

item.title: hologram
item.description: Cuts a hologram out of acrylic using the Hacklab TO laser cutter.

item.title: the camera rotates with the minute hand (animated)
Fuzzy Match Utils
item.description: A collection of string matching algorithms designed with React Select in mind.

item.title: New blog theme
item.description:
Decided to finally try out material design.

(Subtle effect on mousemove)

item.title: Multi-line lists with item separators (CSS only)
Sorta
item.description:
Manually sort a list of items using Timsort.

Abandoned UI experiment; seeing a large list is useful for large sets. Also, quicksort would work better here.

item.title: Node-TimSort Async
item.description: Asynchronous TimSort implementation for Node.js.

item.title: write
item.description: A chill place to write things.

item.title: Color screen
item.description: Full-screen hex, hsl, and rgb color picker & converter

item.title: Crypto Christmas
item.description:
Improve your online privacy and security over the 12 days of Cryptmas.

I was interviewed for this work by NPR, the CBC, and PCMag.

item.title: ditter
item.description: Create a private Twitter list out of someone else’s feed so you can read it.

item.title: RileyJShaw.???
item.description: A single program that outputs my name when you run it through three separate programming languages.

item.title: Vintage
item.description: wallpaper…?

item.title: git tidy-up
item.description: Git CLI to tidy up a working directory with too much going on.

item.title: Flutter
item.description: If you make a pin out of this please send me one.

item.title: Superformula explorer
item.description:
“…the formula can be used to describe many complex shapes and curves that are found in nature. Gielis holds a patent related to the synthesis of patterns generated by the superformula.”

:facepalm:

item.title: Cyber pilot (with controls)
item.description: Play this one in a small window. Arrow keys to move around.

item.title: Ring
item.description: It was left here by the aliens.

item.title: Bubble
item.description: Follows your mouse.

item.title: w h a t
item.description: Short narrative experiment written for the PICO-8 fantasy console.

item.title: Countdown button
item.description: Autoplay is a scourge…

item.title: Blinds
item.description: It resizes!

item.title: Blinkers
item.description: Threw this together to help with color inspiration for another project.

item.title: NSA Ipsum
item.description: An NPM module containing words from the NSA Analyst’s Desktop Binder, Section 2.13: “Key Words & Search Terms”.

item.title: Flubber
item.description: Nobody’s perfect! Takes a MIDI file and messes up a few of the notes.

item.title: Virtuoso
item.description:
Play a .mid file at your own speed using a MIDI keyboard!

Works best with MIDI keyboards, but you can use your computer keyboard too.

item.title: average-color
item.description: Returns the average color from a list of HSL arrays.

item.title: Plaster swirls, sort of
item.description: I’ve got plaster swirls on my ceiling. This began as a recreation of that and turned into something else.

item.title: Synthesia
item.description: Synthesizer synesthesia. Turn MIDI notes into colors.

item.title: Spokes, etc
item.description: Geometric patterns created from straight lines cutting through a circle.

item.title: Spotlight
item.description: Move your mouse / finger. Remix of “filings”.

item.title: Keyboard keyboard
item.description:
Use a MIDI keyboard as your laptop keyboard.

Hard press = UPPERCASE, soft press = lowercase.

item.title: Typing for two
item.description: A competitive typing tutor. Two players type different paragraphs on the same keyboard, simultaneously.

item.title: Free
item.description: Poster? Coaster?

item.title: Falling
item.description: Looping CSS animation.

item.title: Single line tiling
item.description:
Tiling patterns from a single spiral with a fixed modulation signal.

Though the line’s formula stays consistent as it spirals out, the tile grid doesn’t increase in size.

item.title: Shell or something
item.description: Stumbled upon this on my way to making swirls.

item.title: swirls
item.description: Abandoned background generator. Pattern is played out.

item.title: Literate theme
item.description: A CLI tool to update your favorite text editor theme. Emphasizes comments and mutes everything else.

item.title: Dandelion
item.description: Independently oscillating points that form a greater picture together.

item.title: Jittery visualizer v3
item.description: V1 -> V2 contained the tiniest tweak perceptually (smooth line all ’round), but it was a technical challenge. V2 -> V3 contains the tiniest code tweaks, but is perceptually very different. Uses canvas transform logic from zonin’.

item.title: zonin’
item.description: Efficiently scales and rotates canvas image data seemingly in-place using two overlapping canvas elements.

item.title: Have you seen my ship?
item.description: It’s hard to see the ship… :/

item.title: Circling circles
item.description:
Very simple rule applied to a bunch of particles for a smooth ripple effect.

Changing the timing function on this one is extremely satisfying.

item.title: Jittery visualizer v2
item.description: Added a linear ramp and smoothing function to v1 to make the lowest frequency bin match up with the highest frequency bin in both position and derivative. The effect is a continuous circle, versus the “speech-bubble” notch effect of v1.

item.title: Stepping down a corridor
item.description: Trying out CSS animation steps().

item.title: slant + swap + shift
item.description: Playing with bg gradient dots, CSS only.

item.title: sway + swell + spin
item.description: Playing with bg gradient stripes, CSS only.

item.title: Typelab
item.description: Repeatedly apply a set of transformations to text.

item.title: Function cards
item.description: Simple visualizations of sinusoidal functions.

item.title: XOR profile card
item.description: Accidentally discovered this 2d water effect by stacking sine waves. Simple but convincing!

item.title: Desire paths
item.description: Remix of ~~~ using angles & moving agents instead of absolute positions.

item.title: filings
item.description: Cliché vector “field” “diagram”.

item.title: Look closer
item.description: CSS-only typography experiment.

item.title: Lattice 3
item.description: A tricker, more generalized case of Lattice implemented on a trangular grid.

item.title: Lattice
item.description: Lines + circles.
Blend modes + simplex noise.

item.title: Cyber pilot
item.description:
Title screen born of nostalgia for the pictures we used to draw on oscilloscopes.

n͖͙͍͔o̴̰̤͈͙̘̥w͉̮ ̩w̺̘i̠̲͉̘̪t̟̜͉͓͈͞h̩͚̺̕ ̧͉̹̮̠ͅm̻̰͔̜͚o̲̥̘r̶͕̟͇͇̟̭e̠ ̩̺̖̟g̪̬̰̺̟̪͈͜l͇͉͚̬͞i̡ṭ̠̲̰̦ͅc̨̣ͅh̰̲̯̺͕̼͈͜e̷͕̜͎̱s̢̩

item.title: Canvas compositing operations
item.description: A quick look into some of the options for context.globalCompositeOperation.

item.title: snowballs!
item.description: Async/await ftw

item.title: Stormy green
item.description: Sine waves shifting sinusoidally.

item.title: Stormy green (ripples)
item.description: A version of Stormy Green that doesn’t drift out of phase.
Variations on Stone with controlled line angles across the circle.

item.title: Stone
item.description:
Sphere effect arising from random lines across a circle.

I’m sure someone else discovered this effect long before I did.

item.title: Arcs
item.description:
A little doodle with a lot of circles.

Could be done far more efficiently by scaling / translating a fixed amount of concentric circles, but math-ing it was more fun :)

item.title: Smart-splitting window panes (CSS only)
item.description:
If the window is wider than it is tall, it’ll split into columns. Otherwise, it’ll give you two rows.

This layout was rather difficult before flexbox came along.

item.title: Rows of ’em
item.description:
There’s nothing like driving by a field of trees in the passenger seat.

Super quick demo trying (failing, mostly) to capture the perspective effect.

item.title: Astronomie & Astrophysique
item.description: Small canvas demo using Lissajous curves.

item.title: Bell curves
item.description: Hey! It’s a normal distribution!

item.title: Groovy!
item.description: Animated lava lamps with simple non-rectangular collision detection and metaballs for wax clumps.

item.title: NoJS edge-adaptive tooltip
item.description: CSS Flexbox solution to prevent tooltips from overflowing their container at the edges.

item.title: Mechanical waves
item.description:
Possible header for “Mechanical waves and sound” for Khan Academy’s new content redesign.

Includes a few variations.

item.title: Fadey text backdrop
item.description: CSS technique to make a semi-transparent background fade in perfectly with its box-shadow.

item.title: Joy Division: Unknown Pleasures
item.description:
Iconic album art, animated with a Gaussian function and some simplex noise.

Drag your mouse over it, and click for something special.

item.title: render
item.description: A color-theory game completed as a bonus project for Khan Academy’s 2016 Hackathon. Built with a friend using Unity 3D.

item.title: Titania
item.description: Contribution to the publication “Codrops” for their Line Menu Styles collection.

item.title: whoami.py
item.description:
My contribution to a collection of poems.

From the project page: “there is confusion in the mixed layers of metaphors. This is not an obfuscated code repository; not really. In some ways, it is the opposite.”

item.title: Easy Inbox Zero
item.description: A browser extension that removes the unread count from your Gmail inbox and title bar.

item.title: 2d roll effect experiments
item.description: Attempts to make a believable enough 3d ball-rolling effect using 2d canvas primitives.

item.title: Eye sea
item.description: These used to be pokéballs.

item.title: Gotta catch ’em all
item.description: Riffing on pokéball wave.

item.title: Pokéball wave
item.description: Animated demo written alongside a new programmer who was really into Pokémon.

item.title: Pride and Prejudice (webkit only)
item.description:
Pride and Prejudice in its entirety, sorted by the visual weight of its characters.

I was hoping for a more convincing gradient; the weight calculation could use some work.

item.title: boom!
item.description:
A cross-browser WebExtension to declutter busy pages and make them readable again.

Written originally as a bookmarklet in 2013.

item.title: Dark Theme Everywhere
item.description: A forcefully applied dark theme for the web.

item.title: Tony Montana
item.description:
Nothing gonna stop you, there’s nothing that strong ♫

An excuse to write git push it to-the-limit into the console.

item.title: Pond
item.description: An ASCII art pond ripple animation.

item.title: XOXO Bingo
item.description:
Bingo cards generated from the attendance list of XOXOFest. The first person to meet enough attendees “won” the party.

Built hastily between speakers on the last day of the festival; requires login with Twitter to protect attendees’ identity.

item.title: Rings
item.description: A quick visual built during lunch, inspired by the base of the lunch table.

item.title: .supermac
item.description: A collection of dotfiles that I use + some scripts to keep ’em in sync.

item.title: A responsive one-line header
item.description:
Maintains its default font-size until it is too wide to fit on the page. If it starts overflowing, it will adjust to take up 100% of the page width.

Requires a bit of JS on page load. Just CSS and SVG after that.

item.title: Touchtone math facts
item.description:
A math facts app that you can play from a payphone.

Used to live at the epic phone numbers +1.480.360.MATH (US) and +1.647.360.MATH (Canada).

item.title: Solar static
item.description:
A visual demo using typed arrays.

Creates a shadow based on mouse position.

item.title: SpeeDOMeter
item.description: A speedometer that travels through time with Doc Brown.

item.title: Window Shopper
item.description: A Chrome extension: count and list all page-defined global variables.

item.title: Sliding radio button menu
item.description: A sliding menu concept using just radio buttons and CSS.

item.title: Waveform visualizer
item.description: Boring minimal audio visualizer.

item.title: Jittery shapes
item.description:
Animated shapes with wavelike edges.

Includes an audio visualizer built with the Web Audio API.

item.title: Internal link offset (sticky header)
item.description: Sticky headers cover internally-linked content by default. The most robust solutions I’ve seen use JS, but these 4 lines of CSS seem to do the trick.

item.title: Shepard-Risset Glissando
item.description:
Auditory illusion of a tone that continually descends in pitch. It has been described as a “sonic barber’s pole”.

Implemented with the Web Audio API using custom oscillators.

item.title: Fluid simulation
item.description: Jeff Thomas’s Fluid Simulation, ported to Khan Academy’s CS platform.

item.title: Orbit
item.description: A game: hit space over the red dots to earn points.

item.title: Challenger
item.description: Pop-up programming challenges in your browser.

item.title: drawRotatedRect
item.description: Two versions of a canvas util function.

item.title: Meta Letters
item.description: The letters [A-z], composed of smaller versions of themselves.

item.title: 28 Hour Day
item.description: A Pebble watchface that divides the week into six 28-hour days instead of the standard 24/7.

item.title: Slidey menu
item.description:
A quick ~10m demo to demonstrate some animation concepts.

Probably doesn’t work cross-browser.

item.title: Flexbox chat interface
item.description:
Responsive chat interface using CSS flexbox.

Created as a proof of concept for TextSecure’s browser extension layout.

item.title: Hackers.cool
item.description:
Retro command line emulator using my TinyTerm library.

Type “help” for command list.

item.title: Console
item.description: Functional JavaScript terminal using my TinyTerm library.

item.title: Tinyterm
item.description: A tiny in-browser terminal.

item.title: Typographic HUSL sweep
item.description:
Since I made a fancy headline for Terra I figured it was only fair to make one for sweep.

Never used.

item.title: Newspaper column
item.description: Web typography experiment: drop cap + justify.

item.title: Fingerprint
item.description: In-browser keystroke dynamics demo using Tappy.

item.title: Tilted type
item.description: Web typography experiment using CSS transforms and SVG.

item.title: Tappy
item.description: A JavaScript library for recording and comparing rhythms

item.title: Multi-level spoilers
item.description:
UI pattern for nested spoilers.

Demo involved patching Adobe Web Platform’s balance-text polyfill.

item.title: Folding tutorial concept
item.description: UI experiment for line-by-line tutorials. Annotations can be turned off to expose a complete code example.

item.title: My website fits in a favicon
Text fade-in effect
item.description: A bit of an anti-pattern, but people seemed to like it.

item.title: Moore / Von Neumann neighborhoods
item.description: Utils for Terra.

item.title: Tic Attack Toe
item.description:
Rapidfire, turnless tic-tac-toe with a custom websocket lobby.

Inspired by Kung-Fu Chess.

item.title: Reaction
item.description:
Reaction game that steals your history*. It exploits alpha-rounding to create an n-input OR gate for CSS :visited states, then probes links in sequential binary trees.

*This is a proof-of-concept and won’t actually steal anything.

item.title: Racer
item.description:
3D race-to-the-finish game.

Built with a custom raycaster / collision engine.

item.title: Tappy game
item.description: Tap the rhythm of the lyrics displayed.

item.title: Film-grain effect in 117B of JavaScript
item.description: Warning: contains a blocking loop that may cause browsers to hang briefly.

item.title: Funky wallpaper in 106B of JavaScript
item.description: Warning: contains a blocking loop that may cause browsers to hang briefly.

item.title: Funky wallpaper with blur effect in 111B of JavaScript
item.description: Warning: contains a blocking loop that may cause browsers to hang briefly.

item.title: Material design ripple effect
item.description: I needed this for tappy before there were 1000 libraries to choose from.

item.title: Super slinky
item.description:
No jQuery dependency, section progress shown in headers, click to scroll, resizable with scrollbar on window. Inspired by Slinky.js and DesignWall.

Currently doesn’t work on iOS < 8 since they mess with scroll events. If you use this in production, look into iScroll.

item.title: Short domain finder
item.description: Search for emoji domains and short domains.

item.title: Pairjam
item.description: Realtime web-based collaborative coding.

item.title: Everything button
item.description: Initial pass at the menu system for Pairjam.

item.title: CSS tab bar
item.description: For Pairjam.

item.title: Expanding user circles
item.description:
For Pairjam.

Bad interaction tbh.

item.title: Face
item.description: Displays a random ASCII face.

item.title: “Points” color palette
item.description: I was designing an app with a rainbow interface, so I threw this page together to see how the palette looked together.

item.title: Placid tab
item.description: A minimal New Tab page for Chrome

item.title: Linescapes
item.description: An audiovisual experiment that generates landscapes from sound waves.

item.title: Graphing depth
item.description:
Pretty images from math.

Supports my blog post of the same name.

item.title: Reduce Background Flicker
item.description: A Chrome extension to preserve the background color between page loads.

item.title: n-input OR gate in CSS
item.description:
Used to work. Doesn’t anymore.

Part of “Stealing history with CSS binary trees”.

item.title: Thoughtboard
item.description: Democratic billboard for sharing the pulse of a community. Popular thoughts will be displayed on the billboard for all to see.

item.title: Glorious pattern matcher
item.description: Takes a series of characters and a series of words and checks if they match the same sequence. Words need not be delimited.

item.title: filesupply
item.description: WebRTC file sharing concept thrown together in ~40 minutes (or your pizza’s free!)

item.title: A disco badass
item.description: How I like to think of myself.

item.title: Wind alarm
item.description: Only wakes you up if the wind is howling. An alarm for kiteboarders.

item.title: Ben Eath: Surf Ace
item.description:
A multiplayer shark-frenzy controlled with your phone.

Created with friends at Hacker School. I was in charge of the mobile + WebSocket controller framework and the server.

This used to be on Chrome Experiments but it’s offline now :(

item.title: Key Generator
item.description:
A super-efficient memoized key generator in JavaScript.

Lowercase alphanumeric, length [1, 198).

item.title: Mirror
item.description: Small demo of live translation synchronization.

item.title: Thin diagonal stripes
item.description:
CSS gradient rule for thin diagonal lines.

Cross-device, cross-browser.

item.title: Fastdraw
item.description: Checks how quickly you can navigate to other sites in a new tab. Only works in Firefox.

item.title: Sweep
item.description: A JavaScript library for smoother color transitions

item.title: Own this website
item.description: An experimental online game of King of the Hill, on this very website.

item.title: Tiny boxes
item.description: Chat through happenstance. The interface adapts to meet demand.

item.title: Mean tone
item.description: Supporting code for my “Taking the average tone” blog post.

item.title: Pursuit
item.description: CSS animation with no additional HTML or JS.

item.title: Tiny TCP
item.description: Really little chat server written in Node.js.
A terrible clone of a terrible clone of a game.

item.title: Important people
item.description: Scrapes Wikipedia to find the most important births and deaths of each year by word count.

item.title: Composer
item.description: Generate frequencies, chords, and songs.

item.title: terra
item.description: A JavaScript library for simple biological simulations and cellular automata.

item.title: Ushld
item.description: Tell people on the internet what they should do.

item.title: Strobe
item.description: Boring.

item.title: Space avoiders
item.description: Thx Mary :)

item.title: Nite versions
item.description: Recreating Soulwax’s album art in CSS.

item.title: CSS gradient blocker
item.description: Using a repeating pattern to give the illusion of semi-transparency.

item.title: Zig-zag text effect
item.description:
CSS only.

Fun with gradients and background-clip: text.

item.title: Watercolor
item.description: Uses Chrome’s canvas compositing algorithm (circa 2013) to simulate watercolor painting in the browser.

item.title: Simple slide-out footer
item.description: Slide-out footer with semantic markup.

item.title: Canvas spectrum
item.description: lol these variable names…

item.title: COS logo
item.description: Recreating a popular clothing brand’s logo in CSS.

item.title: 3D text!
item.description: BYOG (glasses).

item.title: Vanishify
item.description: Sass vanishing point mixin.

item.title: Ball
item.description: Simple animation in three.js.

item.title: Chamber
item.description: Random mutations of a polyhedron.

item.title: Speedy scroll
item.description:
Block pointer events on scroll to reach 60fps.

A follow-up article has since been written to show more robust techniques.

item.title: Minimal fizzbuzz
item.description: Two very small implementations of Fizzbuzz: one in JavaScript (74B) and one in Stylus (126B).

item.title: Flower power
item.description: Sass flower with variable number of petals.

item.title: CSS slope illusion in <140 characters
item.description: Are the base lines straight or at an angle?

item.title: Focus
item.description: Linear gradient, animation, and background-clip

item.title: Wacky static 2: TV edition
item.description: Used this as my 404 page for a while.

item.title: Planets
item.description: Pure Sass animated solar system.

item.title: 2p Snake
item.description: Snake for 2 players on the same keyboard?! Saucy.

item.title: Sass animation grid
item.description: Collection of Sass animations.

item.title: Fifty shades of grey
item.description: Ha.

item.title: FS video + blocking tiles
item.description: Recreating a nice hover effect in CSS.

item.title: Infinite burger
item.description: Pure Sass hamburger. Sesame-seed bun, lettuce, tomato, fresh cheddar, and infinity perfectly cooked Angus beef patties.

item.title: DNS error easter egg
item.description: Hover over “DNS”.

item.title: Long-shadow cards
item.description: Each one’s only a single element.

item.title: Long-shadow input series
item.description: An attempt to give flat buttons some depth. Collection of inputs including radio buttons, checkboxes, and text inputs.

item.title: Typographic loader
item.description: Loading bar concept.

item.title: Custom carat
item.description: Ugly implementation of something pretty.

item.title: Worst practices
item.description:
Some Javascript that I threw together for a friend to 1) solve his problem, 2) explain some hacky JS tricks, and 3) attempt to show him that they aren’t always great to use since they obfuscate your code.

There’s actually some cool stuff going on in here, so I’ve commented it thoroughly.

item.title: Shadowfy
item.description: Sass 3D text-shadow mixin.

item.title: ASCII loaders
item.description: Geeky loading animations using ASCII characters.

item.title: Color Jump!
item.description:
Sass mixin: jump from x to y in z steps.

This was my first CodePen to hit the front page :)

item.title: Git cheatsheet
item.description: A quick reference for before your morning coffee.

item.title: Frequency bar visualizer
item.description:
A simple frequency bar visualizer for audio in Processing.

Features a terrible mash-up of Watermelon Man and Mos Def’s Beef freestyle that I threw together in Audacity…

item.title: CSS beach
item.description: Geeky loading animations using ASCII characters.

item.title: Perspective buttons
item.description:
Buttons that maintain linear perspective from the screen’s vertical midpoint.

The perspective effect can be added to anything; a site where each content block was extruded could look interesting.

Chrome only as of 2018.

item.title: Nested radio menu
item.description: Semantic vertical menu using nested radio buttons and CSS.

item.title: Phi fo fum
item.description: A SASS template based on the golden ratio.

item.title: Sass tooltips
item.description: Sass speech bubble mixin.

item.title: Instaframe
item.description: Buggy proof of concept exploring the CSS background-attachment property and a 3rd-party Instagram API.

item.title: Peek
item.description: Scrolling gallery view for Tumblr.

item.title: Zenwall
item.description:
A depth-sensing grafitti wall for touch-screens & pen tablets.

Demo built for web browsers and Wacom tablets. Installation involved creating a large infrared plane behind a flexible display and sensing deformations using Community Core Vision.

item.title: FitHub
item.description: A fitness form trainer using an Xbox Kinect.

item.title: Jumble puzzle
item.description: Word-search puzzle generator.

item.title: Terminal display
item.description: Used to promote qhack.ca (defunct) “hack nights” at my university.

item.title: HTML quine
item.description: A quine is a non-empty computer program which takes no input and produces a copy of its own source code as its only output.

item.title: Infiniteye
item.description: My former personal brand mark.

item.title: That’s nuts!
item.description: Settings reveal concept where a nut unscrews itself then drops a curtain.
